headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one word or group of words underlined and one gap. From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence:

S1.qs: 1.	The workers expected management to be ..................... rather than **indifferent** to, their welfare.
2.	Words spoken in the heat of an argument could **offend** rather than ........................
3.	Although they have declared the student a **suspect**, he may turn out to be .....................
4.	Some **outspoken** social critics live a .................. family life
5.	While the manager was **prudent** in his expenditure, his deputy was .....................
6.	The **cultured** and the ............. are known by their conduct
7.	Many Africans now live in abject **poverty** instead of enjoying the ..................... of their countries.
8.	Tunde **seldom** asks questions in class and was ....................... very careful when he did.

S2.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences:

S2.qs: 9.	Tests revealed that the winning athlete had taken performance-enhancing ......................
10.	The lawyer’s client was .................... under oath in the law court
11.	That Fulani man has a large ....................... of cattle.
12.	 The relationship between the couple is ........................ for lack of communication
13.	Many players have complained about biased ..................... by referees.
14.	Government officials have to swear to ................... of secrecy
15.	The students ..................... the planned meeting with the new principal.
16.	She won by ......................... because her opponent refused to play
17.	Some candidates have developed the ...................... for cheating in examinations.
18.	The secretary was ............................ from her job for absenteeism

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 19.	Peter was on tenterhooks until the examination results were announced. This means that Peter was

opts.q2: C. anxiously waiting for the results

S3.qs: 20.	The military leader blazed the trail by handing over power to civilians. This means that the leader

opts.q0: A. people should be willing to compromise
opts.q3: D. any gift should be accepted

S3.qs: 22.	Jumai’s life was in danger but now she has turned the corner. This means that Jumai

opts.q0: A. has given up hope of recovery

S3.qs: 23.	It is unwise for politicians to make off-hand statements to journalists. This means that politicians

opts.q0: A. should insist on their interviews being recorded
opts.q1: B. would be wise not to speak to journalists
opts.q2: C. should not make any statement to journalists
opts.q3: D. should be sure of whatever they say

S3.qs: 24.	The dentist told his patient that the toothache would pass off when he took some aspirins. This means that

opts.q0: A. the tooth would automatically drop off
opts.q2: C. the pains would spread to other teeth

S3.qs: 25.	The students made such a noise in the class that the teacher read them the Riot Act. This means that the teacher

opts.q1: B. instigated the students to riot
opts.q2: C. warned the students to stop the noise or get punished
opts.q3: D. read the school’s rules and regulations to the students

S3.qs: 26.	When I went on leave I asked Mr. Bamson to hold the forte. This means that I asked him to

opts.q3: D. be strong in my absence

S3.qs: 27.	The senator's house is the last word in comfort. This means that the senator

opts.q0: A. has the most comfortable house
opts.q1: B. speaks about the comfort of his house
opts.q2: C. owns the last house on Comfort Street
opts.q3: D. has a house that is not comfortable

S3.qs: 28.	I was given a standing ovation for my outstanding performance. This means that

opts.q0: A. everyone stood up to clap for me
opts.q1: B. everyone stood to catch a glimpse of me
opts.q3: D. I was asked to stand up

S4.headd: From the words lettered A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word or group of words as it is used in the sentence.

S4.qs: 29.	His father’s death **derailed** his plans to enter the university
31.	The students made **scathing** remarks about the vice chancellor
32.	Several cabinet ministers joined the president's **retinue** for the summit conference
33.	The manager refused his deputy’s request in **reprisal** for his disloyalty
34.	She **spurned** the advances of her boss
37.	Don’t expect anything but **hypocrisy** from politicians

S5.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences:

S6.headd: In the following pasages, the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, four options are offered in columns lettered A to D, choose the option that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gaps in the passage.
PA: Last month our school marked her diamond jubilee with a double celebration: an interhouse sports competition in the morning and a football match later in the afternoon. The former was very exciting as many --74-- were broken and several new ones were set.
The most exciting were the --75-- events, especially the 100 metres --76--, the 200 metres hurdles and the --77-- races. The --78-- did not seem particularly interested in the --79-- events.
Expectedly, the football match; drew a very large crowd because of the reputation of the two contesting teams. The --80-- was taken by the State Administrator who was tne special --81--.
Our team played excellent soccer; the --82-- were accurate and our players displayed such --83-- that the spectators were not surpirsed when, by the end of the --84--,

they had scored three goals to nil. In the second half, one of our players was shown a --85-- for a rough --86--. Although our opponents were awarded a --87-- our --88-- was so smart that he did not concede a goal.
